enonce.texte:
Read the text below about a traditional festival in Cameroon and answer the questions that follow.

The Ngondo Festival

The Ngondo festival is a major traditional event celebrated by the Sawa people of the Littoral Region of Cameroon. Last year, my family and I travelled to Douala to participate in this colourful cultural manifestation. We arrived at the beach early in the morning. The atmosphere was vibrant and joyful. Many people wore beautiful traditional clothes. They sang traditional songs and danced to the rhythm of drums. I watched the canoe races on the Wouri River. The canoeists paddled powerfully and the crowds cheered loudly. We ate delicious local food like grilled fish and plantains. The most exciting moment was the traditional wrestling competition. The participants displayed great strength and skill. My uncle, a respected elder, told us the history of the festival. He explained the importance of the river in Sawa traditions. We returned home in the evening, feeling proud and happy to have experienced this unique cultural manifestation.
images: A vibrant illustration of the Ngondo festival in Douala, Cameroon. Show people in traditional Sawa attire, canoe races on the Wouri River, a crowd cheering, and traditional dancers. The atmosphere should be festive and colourful.

questions.texte: Where did the narrator's family travel to participate in the Ngondo festival?
questions.reponse: They travelled to Douala.

questions.texte: List three activities that the narrator saw or participated in at the festival.
questions.reponse:
1. Canoe races on the Wouri River.
2. Traditional dancing to the rhythm of drums.
3. Traditional wrestling competition.

questions.texte: According to the text, why is the Wouri River important to the Sawa people?
questions.reponse: The Wouri River is important in Sawa traditions.

questions.texte: What is the main idea of this text?
questions.reponse: The main idea is to describe the narrator's personal experience participating in the Ngondo festival, a key traditional and cultural manifestation of the Sawa people in Cameroon.

intitule: Grammar: Simple Past Tense of Regular Verbs

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the simple past tense of the regular verbs in parentheses.

questions.texte: We ______ (watch) the traditional dance performance with great interest.
questions.reponse: watched

questions.texte: The chief ______ (welcome) the guests warmly.
questions.reponse: welcomed

questions.texte: My sister ______ (carry) a basket of fruits to the ceremony.
questions.reponse: carried

questions.texte: The musicians ______ (play) the drums all night long.
questions.reponse: played

questions.texte: My mother ______ (prepare) a special meal for the festival.
questions.reponse: prepared

questions.texte: The young men ______ (dance) energetically.
questions.reponse: danced

questions.texte: We ______ (stop) at several villages to admire their cultural displays.
questions.reponse: stopped

questions.texte: The festival ______ (last) for three days.
questions.reponse: lasted

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue between Amina and Jean about a cultural event, then complete it with the correct information. Then, create a similar dialogue with a partner.

Complete the Dialogue

Amina: Hi Jean! Did you go to the cultural festival in our village last weekend?
Jean: Yes, I did. It was fantastic!
Amina: Really? What activities did you participate in?
Jean: I ____________ (watch) a traditional wrestling match and I ____________ (listen) to some wonderful folk songs.
Amina: That sounds interesting! Did you try any local food?
Jean: Of course! I ____________ (eat) 'ndolé' and 'koki'. They were delicious.
Amina: ____________ (see) the traditional dancers?
Jean: Yes, I did. They ____________ (perform) the 'Bikutsi' dance. The colourful costumes were amazing!
Amina: It seems you had a great time. I wish I had been there!
Jean: Yes, it was an unforgettable experience. You must come next year.
images: Two teenagers, a boy and a girl, are sitting on a bench in a park, having an animated conversation. The boy is gesturing excitedly, and the girl is listening attentively, smiling. A traditional African drum is placed next to the bench.

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the simple past tense of the verbs in parentheses.
questions.reponse:
Jean: I watched a traditional wrestling match and I listened to some wonderful folk songs.
Jean: Of course! I ate 'ndolé' and 'koki'.
Amina: Did you see the traditional dancers?
Jean: Yes, I did. They performed the 'Bikutsi' dance.

sousQuestions.texte: Based on the dialogue, what did Jean eat?
What dance did the performers do?

questions.texte: Create a short dialogue (at least 6 exchanges) with a partner. One of you asks about a cultural manifestation you visited, and the other answers. Include at least 3 verbs in the simple past tense.
questions.reponse:
Example of expected dialogue (student should produce something similar):
Student A: Hey, did you go to the Nguon festival last month?
Student B: Yes, I did! It was in my grandfather's village.
Student A: What did you see there?
Student B: I watched the traditional dances and listened to the folk tales told by the elders.
Student A: Did you eat anything special?
Student B: Yes, I ate a lot of fresh corn and drank palm wine. It was delicious!

intitule: Writing: A Story about a Cultural Event

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (100-150 words) about a traditional or cultural manifestation you have participated in. Use the simple past tense of regular verbs. Include details like the name of the event, where and when it took place, what you did, and how you felt. You can choose from the following examples: Ngondo Festival (Sawa), Nguon Festival (Bamoun), Festima (Bafoussam), or a traditional wedding or funeral ceremony in your village.
images: A collage of images showing various traditional ceremonies and festivals in Cameroon. Include a masked dancer from the Grassfields, a canoe race, women in traditional attire performing a dance, and a group of drummers.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph about a cultural manifestation you have participated in. Remember to use the simple past tense of regular verbs.
questions.reponse: This is a creative writing task. The answer should be a paragraph of 100-150 words. Example: 'Last year, I visited my uncle in Bafoussam during the Festima festival. I arrived on a Friday afternoon. The whole city was very lively. My cousins and I walked to the main square and watched a large parade. Many dancers performed traditional dances in colourful costumes. We also listened to some famous musicians play modern and traditional music. In the evening, my uncle prepared a delicious meal for us. I enjoyed the food a lot. We returned home late at night. I felt very happy because I discovered many beautiful aspects of the culture of the Western region.'

enonce.texte: Imagine you are talking to a foreign friend who wants to learn about Cameroonian traditions. Answer the following questions with complete sentences. Use the vocabulary related to cultural manifestations and the simple past tense if needed.
images: A map of Cameroon highlighting the regions and indicating the location of major cultural groups like Sawa, Bamoun, Fulani, and Bantu.

questions.texte: What is the most important traditional festival in your region?
questions.reponse: Student's answer should name a festival specific to their region. Example: 'The most important traditional festival in my region is the Nguon festival.'

questions.texte: What do people wear during this festival?
questions.reponse: Student's answer should describe the traditional attire. Example: 'People wear beautiful Ndop cloth or colourful traditional outfits with beads and cowries.'

questions.texte: Name one traditional activity that takes place during this festival.
questions.reponse: Student's answer should name an activity. Example: 'One traditional activity that takes place during the festival is a horse race.'

questions.texte: Why are these cultural manifestations important for Cameroonian people?
questions.reponse: Student's answer should reflect understanding of cultural importance. Example: 'These cultural manifestations are important because they help us preserve our traditions, unite our communities, and teach young people about our history and customs.'

intitule: Listening and Speaking: Describing a Cultural Celebration

enonce.texte:
Listen to the recording of someone describing the 'Nguon' festival. Then, answer the questions and use the information to describe the festival to a partner.

Transcript of the Recording

"Good morning, everyone. Today, I want to share with you my experience at the Nguon festival. The Nguon festival is a very important cultural celebration for the Bamoun people in the West region of Cameroon. I visited Foumban, the capital of the Bamoun kingdom, during the festival two years ago. The celebration took place in the Royal Palace and its surroundings. Many Bamoun people wore beautiful traditional clothes with colourful beads. The Sultan, the traditional ruler, led a grand procession. The people danced and played local musical instruments. I watched the artisans create their crafts, from woodcarving to weaving. The most fascinating part was the blessing of the harvest. They prayed for a good harvest for the year. It was a wonderful experience that taught me a lot about Cameroonian culture."
images: A photograph of the Sultan's Palace in Foumban, Cameroon, with people dressed in traditional Bamoun attire gathering in front for a celebration. Include details of the distinctive architecture and colourful clothing.

questions.texte: What is the Nguon festival?
questions.reponse: It is a very important cultural celebration for the Bamoun people.

questions.texte: Where did the celebration take place?
questions.reponse: It took place in the Royal Palace and its surroundings in Foumban.

questions.texte: What were two activities the person saw at the festival?
questions.reponse:
1. The people danced and played local musical instruments.
2. The artisans created their crafts (woodcarving and weaving).

questions.texte: Based on the text, why is the Nguon festival important?
questions.reponse: It is important because it is a celebration of their cultural identity, it unites the Bamoun people, it gives thanks for the harvest, and teaches about their history and customs.
